vulnerabilities.title: Hikvision Authentication Bypass
vulnerabilities.description: Hikvision devices with firmware versions 5.4.5 and earlier contain an unspecified authentication bypass vulnerability. An attacker can bypass password verification and gain unauthorized access.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update firmware to version 5.4.6 or later. Contact Hikvision support for firmware updates.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check HTTP Server header for version <= 5.4.5, check for password bypass endpoint

vulnerabilities.title: Hikvision Command Injection in Web Interface
vulnerabilities.description: Hikvision cameras and DVRs contain a command injection vulnerability in the web interface. An attacker can execute arbitrary commands through specially crafted requests.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to firmware version 5.5.82 or later. Apply security patches from Hikvision.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: POST to /SDK/webLanguage endpoint, check for unauthenticated response

vulnerabilities.title: Hikvision Authentication Bypass with Weak Credentials
vulnerabilities.description: Hikvision devices allow authentication bypass using weak or null credentials transmitted in plaintext over HTTP.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update firmware immediately. Use HTTPS instead of HTTP. Enforce strong authentication.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Test HTTP requests with common default credentials

vulnerabilities.title: Hikvision RTSP Protocol Buffer Overflow
vulnerabilities.description: Buffer overflow vulnerability in RTSP protocol handler of Hikvision cameras. Can be exploited to cause denial of service or code execution.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to firmware 8.1.0 or later. Restrict RTSP access.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check RTSP banner version, test with malformed requests

vulnerabilities.title: Hikvision Directory Traversal via Web Interface
vulnerabilities.description: Path traversal vulnerability allowing arbitrary file access through the web interface without authentication.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update firmware to 5.5.85 or later. Restrict web interface access.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Test endpoints like /cgi-bin/../../../../etc/passwd

vulnerabilities.title: Dahua Authentication Bypass
vulnerabilities.description: Dahua DVR/NVR devices contain authentication bypass allowing unauthorized access with default or weak credentials.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to firmware 3.1.1 or later. Change default credentials immediately.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Test with default Dahua credentials (admin/admin, admin/aaaaaa)

vulnerabilities.title: Dahua Command Injection in CGI Scripts
vulnerabilities.description: Multiple CGI scripts in Dahua devices are vulnerable to command injection allowing remote code execution.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update firmware to 3.1.0+. Restrict CGI access.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Test /cgi-bin/configManager.cgi vulnerable endpoints

vulnerabilities.title: Dahua Arbitrary File Upload
vulnerabilities.description: File upload vulnerability allowing unauthenticated upload of arbitrary files to the device.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to latest firmware. Implement upload restrictions.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Test file upload endpoints without authentication

vulnerabilities.title: Dahua Information Disclosure
vulnerabilities.description: Information disclosure vulnerability exposing sensitive system information without authentication.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update firmware. Restrict system information disclosure.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check /etc/board.conf for exposure

vulnerabilities.title: Axis Communications Information Disclosure
vulnerabilities.description: Axis cameras expose version and system information through HTTP headers.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure web server to hide version headers. Use firewall to restrict access.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check HTTP Server headers for version disclosure

vulnerabilities.title: Axis Authentication Bypass in Web Management
vulnerabilities.description: Session handling issue allowing authentication bypass in web management interface.
affected_products: Axis network cameras and video encoders
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to firmware version 10.2.0 or later.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Test session management weaknesses

vulnerabilities.title: Uniview IPC Default Credentials
vulnerabilities.description: Uniview IP cameras contain hardcoded default credentials allowing unauthorized access.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Change default credentials. Use network segmentation.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Test Uniview default credentials

vulnerabilities.title: Telnet Service Enabled on CCTV Device
vulnerabilities.description: Telnet is an unencrypted remote access protocol that transmits credentials in plaintext. Using Telnet on CCTV devices is a critical security risk.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check if port 23 (Telnet) is open
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable Telnet service immediately. Use SSH for secure remote access.

vulnerabilities.title: RTSP Stream Accessible Without Authentication
vulnerabilities.description: RTSP (Real Time Streaming Protocol) video stream is accessible without requiring authentication. Attackers can view live video streams.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Send RTSP DESCRIBE without credentials and check for 200 response
vulnerabilities.remediation: Enable RTSP authentication. Use RTSPS (encrypted RTSP) if available.

vulnerabilities.title: Anonymous FTP Access Enabled
vulnerabilities.description: FTP service allows anonymous access without authentication, permitting unauthorized file transfer.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Connect to FTP and attempt anonymous login
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable FTP anonymous access. Use SFTP instead.

vulnerabilities.title: HTTP Without HTTPS - Credentials Transmitted in Plaintext
vulnerabilities.description: Web interface is accessible over unencrypted HTTP allowing credential interception and man-in-the-middle attacks.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check if port 80 is open and 443 is closed
vulnerabilities.remediation: Enable HTTPS. Configure HTTP to automatically redirect to HTTPS.

vulnerabilities.title: UPnP Service Enabled
vulnerabilities.description: UPnP (Universal Plug and Play) is enabled allowing automatic network traversal which may expose device to the internet.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check if UPnP ports (5000, 1900) are open
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable UPnP if not required. Use firewall to block UPnP ports.

vulnerabilities.title: ONVIF Service Accessible Without Authentication
vulnerabilities.description: ONVIF web service is accessible without authentication allowing device enumeration and potential exploitation.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Send ONVIF GetDeviceInformation and check for manufacturer info
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure ONVIF to require authentication. Restrict ONVIF access to trusted networks.

vulnerabilities.title: SSH Using Weak Cryptography
vulnerabilities.description: SSH service is using weak encryption algorithms or protocols (SSHv1, weak ciphers) vulnerable to attacks.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check SSH version for weak protocols
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update SSH server to latest version. Use only strong ciphers (AES-256, etc).

vulnerabilities.title: Weak SSL/TLS Configuration
vulnerabilities.description: HTTPS is using outdated or weak SSL/TLS protocols (SSLv3, TLS 1.0) or weak cipher suites vulnerable to attacks.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Analyze SSL certificate and supported protocols
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update TLS to 1.2+. Use only strong cipher suites. Disable SSLv3 and TLS 1.0.

vulnerabilities.title: Expired HTTPS Certificate
vulnerabilities.description: HTTPS certificate has expired causing certificate validation warnings and potential man-in-the-middle vulnerabilities.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check certificate expiration date
vulnerabilities.remediation: Renew the HTTPS certificate immediately.

vulnerabilities.title: Self-Signed HTTPS Certificate (Warning Only)
vulnerabilities.description: HTTPS certificate is self-signed not issued by a trusted CA. While not a vulnerability, it indicates the need for proper certificate management.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check if certificate issuer matches a trusted CA
vulnerabilities.remediation: Install a proper certificate from a trusted Certificate Authority.

vulnerabilities.title: Missing HTTP Security Headers
vulnerabilities.description: The web interface is missing critical security headers (HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options) making it vulnerable to attacks.
vulnerabilities.detection_logic: Check for presence of HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure web server to send proper security headers.
